title: threading: fix lost-wakeup race in ThreadPool.notify() on aarch64
crash_site: No crash — a hang. Sporadic ~90s CI timeout / deadlock: a task sits in run_queue, every worker is parked on idle_event, and notified=false. The hang is observed at the caller awaiting the task, with no faulting instruction; only on aarch64.

ground_truth.decoy_fn: the worker park/wait loop (idle_event wait + acquire-CAS clearing notified) and the run_queue push — these look like where the wakeup is lost but are CORRECT; the bug is the relaxed load in notify() on the producer side, a weak-memory-model ordering defect.
ground_truth.fix_summary: ThreadPool.notify() read sync.notified with a relaxed .monotonic load on its fast path. On aarch64's weak memory model this races the worker's acquire-CAS clear of notified plus its relaxed task-queue load: the producer sees a stale notified=true and skips waking a worker, while the worker sees a stale empty queue and parks — stranding a queued task with all workers idle and notified=false (lost wakeup). Fix: replace the relaxed load with self.sync.fetchOr(.{}, .release) so notify() participates in release ordering and observes a coherent notified/queue state.

admission.note: monogram+haiku vs baseline+haiku discrimination. HARD: no crash, just an intermittent aarch64-only hang; the lost wakeup LOOKS like a worker-side wait bug, but the cause is one relaxed atomic load in the producer's notify() (a C11/ARM64 ordering defect). Authored from PR #28907 fix diff (monotonic load -> fetchOr(.release)).

provenance: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/pull/28907 — threading: fix lost-wakeup race in ThreadPool.notify() on aarch64 (merged 2026-04-08); fix commit 770893d28ff9; base 1b009ee4bd1a. Root cause + fix extracted from the commit diff (relaxed .monotonic load -> fetchOr(.{}, .release) in notify()).
